reasoning: Absent search tools, I must rely solely on the provided empirical base rate of 0.422 for FRED questions and the statistical properties of unemployment claims. Unemployment claims generally drift downward or remain flat during prolonged economic expansions resulting in them spending less than half their time increasing. Thus, the generic probability of an increase over a short interval is consistently below 50%, which aligns with the 42.2% base rate. For the shortest horizon (1 week to 2025-11-02), autocorrelation is strongest and the prior base rate applies best, leading to an estimate of 42%. Over longer horizons (4 weeks and 13 weeks), the cumulative variance increases and the likelihood of unexpected macroeconomic shocks pulling the moving average higher becomes stronger, drawing the probability closer to 50%. Therefore, I increase my uncertainty with the forecast horizon, drifting from 0.42 towards 0.49 for the most distant date.
